title: Pan-Seared Snapper with Malaysian Salsa
description: A flavorful and colorful dish featuring pan-seared snapper topped with a zesty Malaysian-inspired salsa. This recipe is perfect for a light and refreshing dinner.
instructions: Season the snapper fillets with salt and chili flakes.
Heat a skillet over medium-high heat and add a tablespoon of oil.
Sear the snapper fillets for 2-3 minutes on each side, then transfer to the oven and bake for 8-10 minutes or until cooked through.
While the snapper is cooking, prepare the Malaysian salsa by combining the red onion, red bell pepper, fish sauce, sushi rice vinegar, and lime juice in a bowl.
Stir in the chopped cilantro and season with salt to taste.
To serve, place a snapper fillet on each plate and top with a spoonful of the Malaysian salsa.
